mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals an abrupt labor market cooling that triggers corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of housing starts and retail sales credit balances indicates a synchronized demand shock that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 800 and RSAFS declines > 2% in 3 months
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 95 on the 2017 base scale reflects robust manufacturing capacity utilization that historically precedes sustained equity risk premium expansions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains levels above 95 for consecutive quarters, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during high-inflation environments.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP consistently outpaces the long-term trend rate, reinforcing corporate profit margins and equity valuations, which translates directly into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the consumer price index exceeding 1.5 percentage points over a six-month period forces market participants to price in aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market expansion that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises > 0.5pp in 3 months and UNRATE falls > 0.2pp in 3 months
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict returns with a Rank IC greater than 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 850 thousand units signals a sharp contraction in residential investment that historically leads broader economic slowdowns, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or positive when HOUST sustains levels below 850 for consecutive months, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during low-interest-rate environments.
